history.thought: The first step in any penetration test is to perform a basic scan to identify open ports and services. Nmap is the standard tool for this. I'll start with a common nmap scan that includes service version detection (-sV), OS detection (-O), and default scripts (-sC) against the target IP.

The previous nmap command failed due to requiring root privileges for OS detection. I need to re-run nmap without the -O flag to gather basic port and service information. Service version detection and default scripts are still useful for initial reconnaissance.
